summary: Tested governance & control features (iteration 25): DeleteWithJustificationModal, ConfirmChangesModal with Diff, Auditoría reactive search. Backend (6/6 tests passed) and Frontend (all 3 flows verified via Playwright) PASS. Minor React DOM-nesting warning in DeleteWithJustificationModal and a missing key prop in Auditoria TableBody were observed in console.

issues:
React DOM nesting warning: renders as

, but the children include another

(line 74) and a

(line 77). This triggers 'In HTML,

/

cannot be a descendant of

' hydration warnings. Replace AlertDialogDescription's children with inline elements or use a wrapper

outside the Description.

React key warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' from TableBody — the .map() of filteredHistorial likely renders something (a fragment or sibling row) without a unique key. Verify the row key around lines 408-... ensures key={item.id}.

passed_tests: Backend: Deletion creates audit entry with justificacion_borrado field populated in /api/historial
Frontend: DeleteWithJustificationModal opens, Confirm button disabled when input empty
Frontend: DeleteWithJustificationModal Confirm button disabled when justification < 10 chars ('short')
Frontend: DeleteWithJustificationModal Confirm button enabled when justification >= 10 chars
Frontend: ConfirmChangesModal appears when 'Guardar Cambios' clicked after modifying a vulnerability
Frontend: diff-field-vulnerabilidad rendered with valor anterior (red) and nuevo valor (green) — Diff visual confirmed
Frontend: cancel-changes-btn closes ConfirmChangesModal and keeps user in edit dialog
Frontend: search-auditoria input filters reactively (20 rows → 14 rows when typing 'eliminar')
Frontend: Audit log displays 'Razón: ...' (justificacion_borrado) under ELIMINAR entries — end-to-end registration confirmed
action_items:
Fix DOM-nesting warning in /app/frontend/src/components/DeleteWithJustificationModal.jsx: AlertDialogDescription wraps a

tag, so the inner

/

elements (lines 74-86) cause invalid nested HTML. Suggested fix: move the
and second

outside AlertDialogDescription, or replace AlertDialogDescription content with only inline elements (e.g., s)
Fix React key warning in /app/frontend/src/pages/Auditoria.jsx TableBody — ensure every direct child of the map() has a unique key (likely a stray <></> or sibling without key around line 408)
critical_code_review_comments:
DeleteWithJustificationModal.jsx: AlertDialogDescription should not contain block elements (

,

). Refactor to keep description as a short single-line string and place the entity badge + warning paragraph as siblings outside the Description.
ConfirmChangesModal.jsx: IGNORED_FIELDS list at line 40 is local to component — consider exposing it via prop so callers can extend if needed (e.g., to ignore organization-specific computed fields).
Auditoria.jsx: filteredHistorial is recomputed on every render without useMemo — for 462 records the cost is low, but if list grows, wrap with useMemo([historial, searchText]).

Backend test_delete_justification.py is well structured; recommend adding similar coverage for /api/config/aplicaciones, /api/config/informes-pentest, /api/config/dominios, /api/config/controles, /api/catalogo-riesgos, /api/hallazgos-auditoria to ensure ALL delete endpoints enforce the 10-char rule (problem statement says 'CUALQUIER elemento').
seed_data_creation: Backend test fixtures created and cleaned up: TEST_DEL_<ts> vulnerability, TestInst_<ts> instituciones, AuditTest_<ts> proveedores (all auto-deleted with justification). One vulnerability 'Open SMTP relay de forma no autenticada' was edited via UI and changes were CANCELLED (not saved), so DB state unchanged.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 25 governance features (delete justification, diff confirm, auditoria search) are fully working. Two minor React warnings remain (DOM nesting in DeleteWithJustificationModal, missing key in Auditoria TableBody) — both are non-blocking but should be fixed for code quality. Backend test suite to run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_delete_justification.py. Note that backend currently enforces min_length=10 via FastAPI Query validator on /api/vulnerabilidades and /api/config/instituciones and /api/config/proveedores — verify other resource DELETE endpoints (aplicaciones, informes-pentest, dominios, controles, catalogo-riesgos, hallazgos-auditoria) have the same Query(min_length=10) constraint applied. The Diff modal only triggers on EDIT (not CREATE) — verify if needed.
